rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L MITCHELL et al VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Reconsideration Of Crown Equipment Corporations Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFFs DANIEL MITCHELL and BRENDA MITCHELL'S MOTION For Reconsideration Of Crown Equipment Corporation's Motion For Summary Judgment IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LVNV FUNDING LLC VS. TIMOTHY C. JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Set Aside Default And/Or Default Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 473, 473.5
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT TIMOTHY JOHNSON'S MOTION To Set Aside Default And/Or Default Judgment Pursuant To C.C.P. SECTIONS 473 AND 473.5 IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 7, 2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT BOTH PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE ANOTHER SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD C KIRCHNER et al VS. DESIGN DEVELOPMENT GROUP PACIFIC VI, LLC A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Good Faith Determination
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT KOLBE & KOLBE MILLWORK CO., INC.'S Motion For Good Faith Determination IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS DEMONSTRATED THAT ITS LIABILITY IS MINIMAL AND THE ECONOMIC VALUE OF THE SETTLEMENT IS FAIR AND DOES NOT PREJUDICE ANY OF THE OTHER PARTIES. =(302/PJM/X-CL)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD C KIRCHNER et al VS. DESIGN DEVELOPMENT GROUP PACIFIC VI, LLC A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing Date [ Defendant Nancy Smith Construction, Inc.'S "Joinder" In Defendant Nancy Wyatt'S Opposition To Kolbe & Kolbe'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 3. DEFENDANT NANCY SMITH CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S Defendant Nancy Smith Construction, Inc.'S "Joinder" In Defendant Nancy Wyatt'S Opposition To Kolbe & Kolbe'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement IS DENIED. =(302/PJM/X-CL)

rulings.Case Title: LISA RAYA VS. LARK CREEK INN PARTNERS L.P. AKA LARK CREED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT LARK CREEK INN PARTNERS, L.P.'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted with 20 days leave to amend. The amended complaint must delete portions of the complaint related to Chubb Services Corporation and Federal Insurance Company; it must allege which provision or provisions, if any, of the workers' compensation scheme require(s) oversight and/or assurance that the employer is properly reporting wages and allege why such provisions are not the exclusive remedy; and it must allege facts demonstrating that the alleged injury is not "collateral to or derivative of" an injury subject to the exclusive remedies of the Workers Compensation Act, and/or that exceptional circumstances warrant compensation and/or the motive behind the alleged wrongful acts violates a fundamental policy of California within the meaning of Charles J. Vacanti, M.D., Inc. v. State Compensation Insurance Fund (2001) 24 Cal.4th 800, 811-812. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSE, L.L.C. VS. LCD INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 5. CROSS-DEFENDANTS UNIVERSE, L.L.C. AND VISIT TAVEETHAMCHAROEN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS UNTIMELY UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 430.40(B). ON THE MERITS, THE ANSWER ADMITS ONLY THAT THE DEFENDANT PROVIDED DESIGN SERVICES, NOT CONTRACTING SERVICES (PARAGRAPH SEVEN). =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA FORSMANN VS. SUTTER HEALTH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN AFFILIATE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT GEOFFREY QUINN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS AS TO THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA FORSMANN VS. SUTTER HEALTH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN AFFILIATE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS MARGARET BILYCK, SUTTER HEALTH, CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER AND COMING HOME HOSPICE'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TWENTY DAYS. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT HAVE A BASIS FOR ALLEGING SURVIVOR CLAIMS. QUIROZ V SEVENTH AVE. CENTER (2006) 140 CAL APP 4TH 1256, 1264. FITZHUGH V GRANADA HEALTHCARE, ETC. (2007) CAL APP 4TH 469. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SYLVIA FORSMANN VS. SUTTER HEALTH, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN AFFILIATE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JANUARY 28, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS MARGARET BILYCK, SUTTER HEALTH, CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER AND COMING HOME HOSPICE'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TWENTY DAYS AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TWENTY DAYS AS TO THE THIRD, FOURTH, SEVENTH AND NINETH CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD SURVIVOR, NOT INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS. =(302/PJM/PB)
